title: Large-wave amplification on periodic discrete lattices: linear theory and a nonlinear extension
description: Paper and full reproducibility layer for the large-wave effect on periodic discrete lattices. Main results: the order law A_N ~ (1/pi) ln N is proved for every N (palindromic prefix-independence lemma); A_N = U_N iff N is prime or a power of two (root-of-unity obstruction); the discrete Schroedinger amplification B_N = Theta(sqrt N) with liminf c0/sqrt2 and a parity-dependent limsup (beta_odd = 0.928..., no elementary closed form); the order law extends to the Dirichlet segment and to ring products C_N x H; and a rigorous chain for the focusing discrete NLS (weak-nonlinear persistence, the modulational-instability band, breather existence and stability). Every quantitative claim is reproduced by a standalone PEP 723 script, with an independent exact-arithmetic / proof layer in GAP, PARI/GP, FriCAS and Rocq.
keywords: vanishing sums of roots of unity
notes: Companion arXiv preprint to be cross-referenced once the identifier is assigned. Fill in the author ORCID before publishing the release.
